items.text: What does the pain feel like, and does it spread anywhere?
choices: Pressure or squeezing radiating to the arm, jaw, or neck
Sharp, positional, pleuritic pain
Sudden tearing pain radiating to the back
Burning, retrosternal pain

items.text: Does the pain come on with activity and go away with rest, or is it there all the time?
choices: Worse with exertion, relieved by rest
Present at rest, unrelated to exertion
Reproducible with chest wall movement or palpation

items.text: Have you been sweating along with the pain?
choices: No diaphoresis
Diaphoresis accompanying the pain

items.text: Have you felt nauseated along with the pain?
choices: No nausea
Nausea accompanying the pain

items.text: Have you felt like you were going to pass out, or did you actually pass out?
choices: No syncope or presyncope
Lightheadedness or presyncope
Frank syncope

items.text: Do you have high blood pressure, diabetes, high cholesterol, or a family history of heart disease, or have you had a heart attack or stent before?
choices: No known coronary risk factors
Multiple risk factors: hypertension, diabetes, hyperlipidemia, smoking, or family history
Known coronary artery disease, prior infarction, or revascularization

items.text: Do you have a known aneurysm, a connective tissue condition like Marfan syndrome, or have you used cocaine recently?
choices: No known dissection risk factor
Uncontrolled hypertension
Known aortic aneurysm, Marfan syndrome, or bicuspid aortic valve
Recent cocaine or stimulant use

items.text: Have you traveled recently, had surgery, been hospitalized, or do you have cancer or a history of blood clots?
choices: No provoking risk factor
Air travel over four hours, surgery, or hospitalization in the past month
Active malignancy or prior venous thromboembolism

items.text: Is the pain worse when you lie flat and better when you lean forward?
choices: No positional change
Worse lying flat, improved leaning forward

items.text: Were you sick with a cold or virus in the past week or two?
choices: No preceding viral illness
Preceded by a viral illness

items.text: Did you do any heavy lifting, start a new exercise, or hurt your chest before this started?
choices: No preceding trauma or unusual exertion
Recent heavy lifting, new exercise, or repetitive strain
Recent direct chest trauma

items.text: Is the pain in a band on just one side, and have you noticed a rash there?
choices: No dermatomal pain
Unilateral band-like pain without a visible rash
Unilateral band-like pain with a vesicular rash

items.text: Is it a burning pain that's worse after eating or lying down, and does it get better with antacids?
choices: No relationship to meals or antacids
Burning worse after meals or lying down, relieved by antacids

items.text: Did you have severe vomiting or retching right before the pain started?
choices: No preceding vomiting or retching
Severe vomiting or retching immediately preceding the pain

items.text: Do you have pain in your right upper belly that spreads to your shoulder or back?
choices: No right upper quadrant or shoulder-radiating pain
Right upper quadrant pain after fatty food
Right upper quadrant pain with fever or chills

items.text: Does this feel like a panic attack, with tingling around your mouth or hands?
choices: No situational trigger or panic history
Situational trigger with paresthesias, resolves with reassurance
Known panic disorder with similar prior episodes

items.text: Have you used cocaine, amphetamines, or other stimulants recently?
choices: No stimulant use
Stimulant use more than 24 hours before onset
Stimulant use within 24 hours of onset

items.text: Measure blood pressure in both arms.
choices: Symmetric blood pressure between arms
Hypertensive on both sides without asymmetry
Blood pressure differential greater than 20 mmHg between arms

items.text: Palpate peripheral pulses for symmetry.
choices: Symmetric pulses throughout
Diminished or absent pulse in one limb

items.text: Auscultate for a new diastolic murmur.
choices: No murmur
New diastolic murmur of aortic regurgitation

items.text: Auscultate for a pericardial friction rub.
choices: No friction rub
Pericardial friction rub present

items.text: Assess jugular venous pressure and heart sounds for tamponade physiology.
choices: Normal jugular venous pressure, normal heart sounds
Elevated jugular venous pressure with muffled heart sounds and hypotension

items.text: Auscultate the lungs for asymmetry or focal findings.
choices: Clear and symmetric bilaterally
Unilateral absent breath sounds with hyperresonance
Focal crackles or dullness
Diminished breath sounds with subcutaneous crepitus or a mediastinal crunch

items.text: Palpate the chest wall for reproducible tenderness.
choices: Nontender, pain not reproduced
Focal chest wall tenderness reproducing the pain
Palpable crepitus or step-off

items.text: Inspect the skin over the painful dermatome for a vesicular rash.
choices: No rash
Vesicular rash in a dermatomal distribution

items.text: Palpate the right upper quadrant for a Murphy sign.
choices: Nontender, negative Murphy sign
Right upper quadrant tenderness with a positive Murphy sign

items.text: Assess mental status and perfusion.
choices: Alert with normal color and perfusion
Diaphoretic, pale, or poorly perfused
Altered mental status with hypotension

items.text: Inspect and palpate the calves for asymmetric swelling or a palpable cord.
choices: Symmetric, no tenderness or swelling
Unilateral swelling, tenderness, or palpable cord
